metadata.title: 3.5-Hour Interview with Luo Fuli: OpenClaw, Agent Paradigm, Post-Training, Org Flattening

conclusion: Luo Fuli, ex-Damo/DeepSeek and Xiaomi MiMo post-training lead, argues the industry has entered the post-training era — her OpenClaw moment came between 2am and 6am on first use, when an agent completed real work overnight. She flattens orgs: shared Mac Minis, group chat, no usage KPIs. GPU splits roughly 3:1:1 research/post-train/inference. Many agent frameworks exist but self-evolving agents remain TBD; post-training needs a pre-training worldview. Investors should watch Anthropic's coding post-train flywheel and GOOGL's ability to align org design with inference economics.
background: In a 217-minute episode, Zhang Xiaojun speaks with Luo Fuli on post-training in the agent age. Luo led post-training at Damo, DeepSeek, and now Xiaomi MiMo. Her OpenClaw moment — first serious overnight agent use from 2am to 6am — mirrors others' ChatGPT moments: interaction shock, not a single paper breakthrough. She advocates flat teams: Mac Minis on desks, shared chat, deliberately no token-usage KPIs. Internal GPU allocation runs roughly 3:1:1 across research, post-training, and inference. Agent frameworks proliferate like clubs, but self-evolving agents are still unsolved. Post-training without pre-training worldview fails — base model priors set the ceiling.
important_facts: OpenClaw moment: Luo's first transformative agent experience ran 2am–6am — autonomous overnight work, not a demo chat. She frames post-training as the binding era after pre-training scale laws plateau; ex-Damo, DeepSeek, and Xiaomi MiMo roles give her cross-China-lab view on RLHF, tool use, and coding agents.

Org design: flat structure with Mac Minis, shared group chat, no usage KPIs — avoids Goodharting token metrics while engineers dogfood agents daily. GPU budget splits ~3:1:1 research/post-train/inference — post-train share rises as agents ship. Many agent framework 'clubs' exist; self-evolving agents that improve without human loops remain TBD.

Technical stack: post-training must inherit pre-training worldview — base model knowledge and safety priors gate agent reliability. GPU ~3:1:1 research/post-train/inference split prices rising post-train spend; investors map Anthropic (Private:Anthropic Long) coding post-train depth versus GOOGL (Watch) balancing Gemini post-train with cloud inference margins.

mental_model.name: Post-Train Era × Flat Org × GPU 3:1:1
mental_model.components: Pre-training sets worldview; post-training sets agent behavior — era shift after scale-law diminishing returns. Flat Mac-Mini orgs dogfood agents without usage KPI distortion. GPU 3:1:1 split prices research, post-train, and inference together — post-train share rises with agent revenue. Self-evolving agents unsolved — human feedback loops still moat.
mental_model.application: Private Anthropic: Long on coding post-train flywheel and Claude Code feedback data. Alphabet (GOOGL): Watch — must fund post-train without starving inference margins; org flattening hard at scale. Agent framework startups: many clubs, few self-evolving winners — bet on data loops, not prompt wrappers.

key_insights.view: Post-training is the era; pre-training is the foundation.
key_insights.question: What changes in Luo's framework?
key_insights.answer: Base models supply worldview and knowledge; post-training supplies agency — tool use, coding, overnight tasks. Without pre-train priors, RLHF and SFT chase shallow behaviors. GPU ~3:1:1 split reflects rising post-train spend as products ship, not just lab papers.

key_insights.view: OpenClaw moment is interaction shock, like ChatGPT.
key_insights.question: Why emphasize 2am–6am first use?
key_insights.answer: Breakthrough felt in autonomous overnight work, not benchmark release — same pattern Su Yu draws for language agents. Product moments lag papers; investors should track dogfood hours and task completion, not parameter counts.

key_insights.view: Self-evolving agents remain TBD amid framework clubs.
key_insights.question: Where is venture edge?
key_insights.answer: Dozens of agent frameworks resemble social clubs — orchestration without compounding learning. True self-evolution needs closed feedback loops (coding fixes, formal proofs). Until solved, post-train teams with flat orgs and real workloads win over wrapper startups.

top_investment_implications.thesis: Alphabet (GOOGL): post-training era raises GPU split toward RLHF/SFT — must align Gemini agent behavior with cloud inference economics and flat-team dogfooding
Anthropic: post-training flywheel via Claude Code real-world feedback — Luo's framework validates coding agents as post-train killer app

golden_quotes: My OpenClaw moment was 2am to 6am — the agent worked while I slept; that is the product.
GPU split is roughly three research, one post-train, one inference — post-train grows as agents ship.
Many agent frameworks are clubs; self-evolving agents are still nobody's solved problem.

events.event: Luo at Damo and DeepSeek; post-training focus
Claude Code terminal form validates harness-over-IDE thesis
Joins Xiaomi MiMo; flat Mac-Mini org design
OpenClaw overnight agent moment; records podcast
Industry shifts GPU budget toward post-train/inference
